contentBlocks.original:
सोमनाथं वैद्यनाथं धन्वंतरिमधाश्विनौ
एतान् संस्मरतः प्रातः व्याधिः स्पर्श न विद्यते
contentBlocks.transliteration:
somanāthaṃ vaidyanāthaṃ dhanvaṃtarimadhāśvinau
etān saṃsmarataḥ prātaḥ vyādhiḥ sparśa na vidyate
contentBlocks.translation: One who remembers Somanātha (Lord Shiva), Vaidyanātha (the healer Shiva), Dhanvantari (the divine physician), and the twin Ashvinis (divine doctors of the Gods) every morning will not be touched by disease.
contentBlocks.meanings: सोमनाथं (somanāthaṃ) – Somanātha (Lord Shiva) (soma:moon | nāthaṃ:lord); वैद्यनाथं (vaidyanāthaṃ) – Vaidyanātha (healer Shiva) (vaidya:physician | nāthaṃ:lord); धन्वंतरिम् (dhanvaṃtarim) – Dhanvantari (divine physician); अधाश्विनौ (adhāśvinau) – and the Ashvinis (adha:and | aśvinau:the Ashvinis); एतान् (etān) – these; संस्मरतः (saṃsmarataḥ) – remembering; प्रातः (prātaḥ) – in the morning; व्याधिः (vyādhiḥ) – disease; स्पर्श (sparśa) – touch; न (na) – not; विद्यते (vidyate) – exists;
